title: 16.-Protiviti Government Services Inc (“Protiviti”): Using General Services Administration (“GSA”) Vehicle for Professional Services Purchase Orders

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to issue purchase orders with Protiviti
for the procurement of professional services using the GSA purchasing vehicle no. GS-
35F-0280X for an amount not to exceed $900,000 through June 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
One-time funding for this project is available in the Department of Information
Technology’s (“IT”) Fiscal Year (FY) 2025 and FY 2026 General Fund allocation as
itemized below.
$450,000 FY 2025: Professional Services
FY 2025 Budget Code: 011-35-363-382-0000-000-472-612990-
(General Fund, IT Department, Professional Services)
$450,000 FY 2026: Professional Services
FY 2026 Budget Code: 011-35-363-382-0000-000-472-612990-
(General Fund, IT Department, Professional Services)
$900,000 Total Professional Services
sections.current_situation:
IT is responsible for managing the City’s network and server infrastructure, cybersecurity,
and enterprise applications. To effectively support and secure these critical systems, IT
requires access to highly specialized expertise. To address gaps in knowledge or
resource availability, IT partners with Protiviti.
In recent years, IT has faced ongoing challenges in recruiting and retaining qualified staff
to maintain essential technology services. This inconsistency in staffing poses a
significant risk to network uptime and overall system stability. Protiviti has consistently
provided skilled resources to help IT “fill the gaps” as needed, ensuring continuity and
reliability of network operations.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@CityofBerkeley.info Website: http://www.CityofBerkeley.info/Manager
Page 2 of 3
Protiviti Government Services: Using GSA Vehicle for Professional Services CONSENT CALENDAR
May 20, 2025
IT leverages purchasing vehicles such as the GSA Master Award Schedules (“MAS”).
These pre-negotiated contracts allow local governments to access competitively priced
products and services, benefiting from volume discounts secured through GSA’s
competitive bid process.
Through this council report, staff are requesting additional spending authority with Protiviti
for specialized professional services through fiscal year 2026. This effort directly supports
the City’s strategic goal to, “Create a resilient, safe, connected, and prepared City.”
sections.background:
In 2019, IT first engaged Protiviti to support cybersecurity initiatives. In 2022, amid the
COVID-19 pandemic and widespread staffing shortages, IT required additional network
and systems support. Protiviti responded swiftly, providing high-quality resources that
enabled IT to transition the City effectively to a hybrid work environment. Since then, IT
has continued to successfully leverage Protiviti’s expertise for network and enterprise
support whenever specialized skills or supplemental resources are needed.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Protiviti will conduct their work remotely thus reducing greenhouse gas emissions from
travel to and from city facilities.
sections.rationale:
The City is currently using Protiviti under multiple GSA vehicles across several
departments for specific professional services engagements. Protiviti works with Robert
Half International to immediately source analysts with the skillset and experience required
to support the City’s critical network and system infrastructure.
Additionally, staff determined that not using Protiviti puts operational support in jeopardy
for key enterprise systems.
sections.alternatives:
Staff considered hiring additional internal staff to fulfill the highly specialized skillset IT
seeks, however, given the current budget constraints of the City, coupled with the deep
technical expertise and knowledge required, staff recommend continuing to partner with
Protiviti.
CONTACT PERSON
Kevin Fong, Director, Information Technology, 510-981-6541
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
PROTIVITI GOVERNMENT SERVICES (“PROTIVITI”): USING GENERAL SERVICES
ADMINISTRATION (“GSA”) VEHICLE FOR PROFESSIONAL SERVICES PURCHASE
ORDERS
WHEREAS, the City has critical network and systems infrastructure that requires a high-
level, specialized skillset; and
WHEREAS, Protiviti has the expertise in operations and implementations of complex
projects, and provides well qualified analysts; and
WHEREAS, the City regularly participates in purchasing vehicles, or Master Award
Schedules (“MAS”) negotiated by GSA, who negotiates long term competitively bid
contracts for prices on supplies, products, and services with suppliers on behalf of
government agencies, including local government, that ensure volume discount pricing;
and
WHEREAS, one-time funding for this project is available in the Department of Information
Technology’s Fiscal Year (FY) 2025 and FY 2026 cost allocation fund, and spending for
this contract in future fiscal years will be subject to Council approval of the proposed
citywide budget and annual appropriation ordinances.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to issue purchase orders with Protiviti to procure professional
services using GSA purchasing vehicle no. GS-35F-0280X for an amount not to exceed
$900,000 through June 30, 2026.
